ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) post-ProxyLogon: w3wp.exe spawning shell/recon binaries (China Chopper-style web shell)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon kill-chain signal — IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) hosting an Exchange OWA/ECP application pool spawning cmd.exe, PowerShell, certutil, net.exe or other recon/LOLBins. This is the post-exploitation behaviour observed after ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065), CVE-2022-41040/41082 (ProxyNotShell) and CVE-2023-21529, where Silk Typhoon dropped China Chopper / ChinaChopper-derived ASPX web shells under \inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client and \Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hash values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MSExchange*" OR Processes.parent_process="*OWA*" OR Processes.parent_process="*ECP*" OR Processes.parent_process="*aspnet_client*" OR Processes.parent_process="*FrontEnd\\HttpProxy*") (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe","procdump.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","makecab.exe")) by host Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename firstTime as firstTime, lastTime as lastTime | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange-on-prem web-shell post-exploit
// w3wp.exe (running an Exchange app pool) spawning shell or recon LOLBins.
let recon_or_shell = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe",
    "ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe",
    "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe",
    "7z.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe",
    "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe",
    "reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
  // Scope to Exchange worker-process app pools — narrows out generic IIS
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (
        "MSExchange", "OWA", "ECP", "FrontEnd", "HttpProxy",
        "aspnet_client", "Exchange Back End")
| where FileName in~ (recon_or_shell)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"     // exclude SYSTEM/computer accounts only when desired
     or InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("system","network service","iusr")
  // SYSTEM-running w3wp spawning cmd.exe is the highest-fidelity HAFNIUM signal
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          AccountName, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange IIS worker spawning shell/recon LOLBins
let recon_or_shell = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe",
    "ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe",
    "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe",
    "7z.exe","rar.exe","makecab.exe",
    "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe",
    "reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                          // process create (4688 + cmdline GPO required)
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
  // Exchange app-pool scoping — w3wp.exe command line carries the AppPoolName
| where CommandLine == "" or true                // CommandLine of child is the suspicious one
| extend ChildBin = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where ChildBin in~ (recon_or_shell)
| where Account !endswith "$"
  or SubjectUserName in~ ("SYSTEM","NETWORK SERVICE","IUSR")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, SubjectUserName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon HAFNIUM Exchange Web Shell Post-Exploit
id: 9b2c4f3a-8a3e-4d8f-9b9a-1e8f2d5a3a01
status: experimental
description: IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) hosting an Exchange app pool spawning shells, recon utilities or LOLBins — canonical HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon / ProxyNotShell post-exploitation.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1057
  - g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_apppool:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchange'
      - 'OWA'
      - 'ECP'
      - 'FrontEnd'
      - 'HttpProxy'
      - 'aspnet_client'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\rar.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_apppool and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Exchange cumulative-update / setup activity (vendor-driven, time-bounded)
  - Admin troubleshooting from inside an Exchange console
level: high
ucs.rationale: This pattern is the most-cited HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon IoC in Microsoft, CISA AA21-062A, Volexity and Mandiant reporting from the 2021 ProxyLogon zero-day window through the 2022/23 ProxyNotShell and OWASSRF campaigns and into 2025 Microsoft reporting that Silk Typhoon expanded into IT-supply-chain Exchange compromises. The combination — w3wp.exe with an Exchange app-pool command line, parent SYSTEM/
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon LSASS dump via procdump and rundll32+comsvcs.dll MiniDump (post-Exchange credential access)
ucs.description: Detects HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon's preferred credential-access path immediately after webshell foothold: dumping LSASS with Sysinternals procdump (often staged to C:\ProgramData\ or C:\Windows\Temp\) or the in-built rundll32.exe comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBin. Microsoft and Mandiant both attributed this exact tradecraft to HAFNIUM following the March 2021 Exchange compromise wave.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as commandline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( (Processes.process_name="procdump.exe" OR Processes.process_name="procdump64.exe" OR Processes.original_file_name="procdump") Processes.process="*lsass*" ) OR ( Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" Processes.process="*MiniDump*" ) OR ( (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe") Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" ) OR ( Processes.process_name="tasklist.exe" Processes.process="*lsass*" Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" ) by host Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — LSASS credential dumping post-Exchange foothold
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
     or InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("system","network service","iusr")
| where
    // 1. Sysinternals procdump targeting lsass (binary may be renamed → check OriginalFileName)
    ( (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
        or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "procdump"
        or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "Sysinternals")
      and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass" )
    // 2. comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBin
    or ( FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
         and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","#24","#-24") )
    // 3. PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump reflective dumper (Out-Minidump / SafetyKatz / Lsassy)
    or ( FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDumpWriteDump","Out-Minidump","Invoke-Mimikatz","sekurlsa","DumpCreds","Get-LsaSecret") )
    // 4. Direct task list of lsass spawned from w3wp — common pre-dump recon
    or ( FileName =~ "tasklist.exe"
         and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass"
         and InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe" )
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          AccountName  = coalesce(AccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountName),
          ParentImage  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent  = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          ChildImage   = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
          ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — LSASS dumping (procdump, comsvcs.dll MiniDump, PS reflective)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
     or SubjectUserName in~ ("SYSTEM","NETWORK SERVICE","IUSR")
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         CmdLow   = tolower(CommandLine)
| where
    ( ChildBin in ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
        and CmdLow has "lsass" )
    or ( ChildBin == "rundll32.exe"
         and CmdLow has "comsvcs.dll"
         and (CmdLow has "minidump" or CmdLow has "#24") )
    or ( ChildBin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
         and (CmdLow has "minidumpwritedump"
              or CmdLow has "out-minidump"
              or CmdLow has "invoke-mimikatz"
              or CmdLow has "sekurlsa") )
    or ( ChildBin == "tasklist.exe"
         and CmdLow has "lsass"
         and ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe" )
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon HAFNIUM LSASS Credential Dump
id: 4f6e3a82-1c2d-4eaa-b5b4-8e8c1f3a72b2
status: experimental
description: HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon-style LSASS dumping — Sysinternals procdump targeting lsass, rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBin, or PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump reflective dumpers — characteristic post-Exchange-webshell credential access.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.011
  - g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  procdump_lsass:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  procdump_renamed:
    OriginalFileName: 'procdump'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  comsvcs_minidump:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
      - 'MiniDump'
  powershell_dump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
      - 'Out-Minidump'
      - 'Invoke-Mimikatz'
      - 'sekurlsa::'
  condition: 1 of procdump_* or comsvcs_minidump or powershell_dump
falsepositives:
  - Authorised IR / forensic dumps by SOC / consultancies (whitelist by signing user / host)
  - Microsoft support requesting an LSASS dump for crash analysis
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's HAFNIUM advisory and the CISA AA21-062A joint alert specifically called out procdump-on-LSASS staged to C:\ProgramData and the rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump technique on compromised Exchange servers. Mandiant's tracking of HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon and the 2024-2025 Microsoft re-attribution to IT-supply-chain attacks both confirm continued use of this dumper combo after Exchange/IIS webshel
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma tag must start with namespace separated with dot from remaining tag.
